relationship_opportunity__risk_assessmen.preference_for_technology_deployment_and: To partner with vendors who provide complete technology support
relationship_opportunity__risk_assessmen.reasons_for_being_unsuccessful_with_pref: Our internal marketing resources are limited, so most of our new business comes from referrals rather than targeted outreach to larger employer groups. We have not invested enough in a dedicated producer or marketing plan focused specifically on 100+ life groups, which is where we would like to grow.

Decision cycles for larger groups tend to be longer and more formal, and we do not yet have a consistent RFP response process or polished presentation materials tailored to HR and CFO stakeholders at that size. As a result, we sometimes get invited late in the process or lose out to agencies that appear more structured.

Our current technology stack is still evolving, and we lack some of the analytics and benchmarking tools that larger employers expect. This makes it harder to differentiate our value beyond personal service, especially when competing against national brokers with robust dashboards and reporting.
relationship_opportunity__risk_assessmen.description_of_how_presenting_new_ideas_: My clients always take my call because they trust me to bring relevant, timely and valuable ideas, products and services to their attention
relationship_opportunity__risk_assessmen.greatest_concerns_line_1: My greatest concern is that as our book of business grows, we may not be able to maintain the high-touch, relationship-driven service that our clients currently rely on, especially during busy renewal seasons and in years with significant regulatory or carrier changes.
relationship_opportunity__risk_assessmen.greatest_concerns_line_2: I also worry about staying ahead of compliance requirements and emerging benefits trends (e.g., mental health support, voluntary benefits, and cost-containment strategies) so that we do not fall behind larger agencies that have in-house compliance teams and dedicated analytics staff.
relationship_opportunity__risk_assessmen.what_you_like_most_about_your_current_si: What I like most about our current situation is the depth of trust we have with our mid-sized employer clients; many of them treat us as an extension of their HR team, and I am often included in strategic planning discussions around retention, culture, and long-term benefits strategy rather than just being brought in at renewal time.

I also appreciate the flexibility we have as an independent agency to choose carrier and vendor partners that genuinely fit each client’s needs, rather than being constrained by rigid corporate mandates, which allows me to be more creative and consultative when designing benefit packages and introducing new technology solutions.
